passages.passage_text: There is a group of six persons in a family: A, B, C, D, E and F. There are two married couples in the family. A is the most talkative in the family while D talks less than E or C. B is not as talkative as D or F. F is more talkative in the family is married to the second most talkative in the family. There are three females and three males. Nobody in the widow or a widower. D is an unmarried male; B is a female. A is of the same sex as the unmarried person other than D. F is married and is not of the same sex as A or married to A. The marital status of the most talkative and the least talkative of the family is the same.

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body: Who is the most talkative member of the family?

questions.body: Which of the following represents a correct couple?

questions.body: Who is the third most talkative in the family?

questions.body: The marital status of F and the least talkative person are:

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body: Which set of statements is sufficient to find out the least talkative person of the family?

choices.c: III, IV, V, VI, VII, VIII
choices.e: All are sufficient for the upcoming
